rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Complaint To Cure Improper Defendant Name Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Amend The Complaint To Add Doe Defendant
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to amend the complaint to cure improper defendant name and alternative motion to amend the complaint to add Doe defendant: Plaintiffs¿ motion to amend the complaint to cure improper defendant name pursuant to C.C.P. section 473(a) is denied. Plaintiffs¿ motion to amend the complaint to substitute Cahill Construction Co. for Doe defendant is granted pursuant to C.C.P. section 474. Plaintiffs have 10 days leave to amend the complaint. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R. GRANT VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect) and off calendar as to issue 3 (premises owner/contractor liability). As to issues 1 and 2, defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Plaintiff has dismissed his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HARRY CARPENTER et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendants Aurora Pump Company'S And Sepco Corporation'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Velan Valve Corp.¿s joinder in Aurora Pump Co. and Sepco Corporation¿s motion to dismiss or stay is denied as moot pursuant to plaintiff¿s notice of dismissal of the entire federal action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
